title: Clone Graph with Random Pointers
description: Given a linked list where each node contains a random pointer that can point to any node in the list or to null, you need to create a deep copy of the list. Each node in the original list has two pointers: next which points to the next node in the list and random which points to any node in the list or null. Your task is to return a deep copy of the list.

examples.explanation: The original list has nodes with values 7, 13, 11, 10, and 1. The random pointers are set as follows: 13 points to the first node (value 7), 11 points to the fourth node (value 10), and so on. The copied list maintains the structure and relationships of the original.
In this case, the first node points to itself with its random pointer, and the second node does the same. Therefore, the deep copy retains these relationships correctly.
Here, the first node has no random pointer, while the second points to the third and the third points back to the second. The cloned list perfectly mirrors these pointers.

constraints: The number of nodes in the linked list is in the range [0, 100].
A node can have any number of edges but will not point to itself.
hints: Consider using a hash map to store the mapping between original nodes and their copies.
You may want to traverse the list twice: once to copy nodes and once to set random pointers.
Pay attention to the edge cases where random pointers may point to null.
Think about how you can use the existing structure of the list to minimize additional space usage.
